steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: The passage opens with “though for different reasons” — signaling that Kuh and Bryson Shahn both received the same award but for distinct reasons. Kuh is described first (art history); the blank must introduce Bryson's contrasting reason (painting). The transition word must signal contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — as a result,: signals causation — Bryson's recognition would be a consequence of Kuh's. That is not the relationship; they are independent recipients honored for different fields. ✗
  • Choice B — for instance,: signals an example of the previous point. Bryson is not an example of Kuh's art history work; she is a separate recipient for a different reason. ✗
  • Choice C — in other words,: signals a restatement or clarification of what was just said. Bryson's painting career is not a restatement of Kuh's art history work. ✗
  • Choice D — by contrast,: signals difference — exactly what “though for different reasons” promised. Bryson is recognized for painting, contrasting with Kuh's art history. ✓
That matches Choice D — by contrast,.
